text: Dreamland is a Birmingham institution and it still earns it. Ribs fall off the bone and that white bread soaking up the sauce is the move. I bring the track parents here after home meets.
Saw's pork with the white sauce is unreal. The greens and mac are exactly what I want after a long Saturday run. Easy 5 stars.
Solid BBQ and those cheese biscuits are dangerous. Close to the clinic so I end up here for a quick lunch more than I should admit.
Good chopped pork plate and the Half Moon cookie is the real reason to come back. Quick service when I'm short on time between patients.
Niki's West is the gold standard for a meat-and-three in this town. Fried snapper, turnip greens, cornbread. I've been coming since I was a kid with my dad.
Green Acres wings and a side of greens hit every time. Cash-friendly, no frills, all flavor. A real Birmingham spot.
Eugene's hot chicken brings the heat. Got the medium and it was plenty. Good post-grill cheat meal on a Sunday.
The Trak Shak is my go-to for running shoes. They actually watch you run and fit you right. Picked up my marathon trainers here before the half.
Resolute knows their stuff. Great gait analysis and they steered me to the right shoe for my mileage. I send all my athletes here.
Good strength sessions and the coaches push you. I cross-train here on clinic days. Wish the early class started a touch earlier for my 5:15 schedule.
Friendly box close to Mom's in Woodlawn. Coaches scale the workouts well and the community is welcoming. Good spot for a guest drop-in.
Oak Mountain is where we host the Cross Country Invitational. The trails are no joke but the views are worth it. Perfect for long-run weekends.
Railroad Park is my favorite city loop for an easy run. Well kept, safe, and the skyline view at sunrise is unbeatable. Brought the team here for recovery jogs.
Biased since I work here, but the care our team gives is the real deal. Dr. Lawson built something special for Birmingham athletes.
My regular Publix right off Crestwood Blvd. Clean, well stocked, and the deli subs are clutch for meal prep Sunday. BOGO weeks keep the grocery bill sane.
O'Henry's is my pre-clinic coffee stop in Homewood. Local roaster, friendly baristas, and right next to the Trak Shak. Start the day right.
Rib It Up out in Ensley does a mean rib sandwich. Old-school, no-nonsense, and the sauce has a kick. Worth the drive across town.
Hattie B's stays packed for a reason. The hot chicken is legit and the pimento mac is a must. Long lines but it moves.
Yo' Mama's brunch is the play after a Saturday long run. The shrimp and grits refuel you right and the staff treats you like family.
My church home. Pastor Hayes preaches with conviction and the men's choir lifts the whole room on 1st and 3rd Sundays. Family every week.
Birmingham institution and it still earns it. Ribs fall off the bone. (Feb 2026)
Cheese biscuits alone are worth the trip. Bring the track parents here. (Feb 2026)
Fast, friendly, and they know my stride — got my long-run shoes dialed. (Feb 2026)
